laws.title: Public Law 16-114 | Amended Ambassadorial Organization and Reporting Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Amends PL 15-102 SEC III(e) to give the President authority to create regional ambassadorships, with notice and Senate-consent language.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; the source is intact and operative, and no later enactment in the reviewed set repeals PL 16-114 itself.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Direct amendment only; no transfer or redesignation chain was resolved.
laws.status_basis: Source is intact and operative. Its only substantive effect is an express amendment to PL 15-102 SEC III(e) with determinable strike-and-insert text. Nothing repeals PL 16-114.

current_implementation.detail: The note is broadly in the right subject area, but it only records the act as a statutory note and does not implement the textual amendment to PL 15-102 SEC III(e).

recommended_actions: Apply the SEC III(e) substitution to PL 15-102.
Replace the boilerplate note with an honest amendment note.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 16-115 | National Security Threat Mandatory Notification to Congressional Leadership Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Creates a notification duty for foreign threats, defines terms, and cross-references 18 U.S.C. 1512 for noncompliance.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; the source is intact and fully operative, and nothing repeals it.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer issue; the cross-reference to 18 U.S.C. 1512 is reference-only, not an amendment.
laws.status_basis: Source is intact and fully operative. It creates a notification duty for foreign threats, defines terms, and ties noncompliance to 18 U.S.C. 1512. Nothing repeals it.

current_implementation.detail: Title 50 is a plausible anchor, but the XML is still boilerplate and does not distinguish the 18 U.S.C. 1512 cross-reference as reference-only. Trello link present.

recommended_actions: Replace the boilerplate note with an honest statutory note under title 50.
Record the 18 U.S.C. 1512 reference as cross-reference only.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 16-116 | Combatant Command Re-establishment Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Repeals the OCJCS Reform Act of 2022 / PL 7-48 and re-establishes six Unified Combatant Commands with command-and-control rules.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; the source is intact and operative, and no later enactment in the reviewed set repeals PL 16-116 itself.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: The enactment is a bundle of direct repeal and re-establishment instructions affecting PL 7-48; no separate transfer or redesignation chain was resolved.
laws.status_basis: Source is intact and operative. It repeals the OCJCS Reform Act of 2022 / PL 7-48 and re-establishes six Unified Combatant Commands with command-and-control rules. Nothing repeals PL 16-116.

current_implementation.detail: The note uses title 50, which is a weak anchor for combatant-command law, and it does not apply the repeal-marking for PL 7-48.

recommended_actions: Apply repeal-marking to the OCJCS Reform Act of 2022 / PL 7-48.
Consider re-anchoring toward title 10 combatant-command provisions.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 16-117 | The Clean Up Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Repeals Senate Joint Resolution 2 and Senate Joint Resolution 3.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; the source is intact and operative, and the repealing resolution itself remains current.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Complete repeal of the targeted joint resolutions; no transfer or redesignation survives.
laws.status_basis: Source is intact and operative. It repeals Senate Joint Resolution 2 and 3; the repealing resolution itself remains current.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note mislabels a current repealing resolution as 'Repealed or superseded' and uses a generic title 50 anchor, but the actual target is uncodified joint resolutions.

recommended_actions: Replace the boilerplate note with a concise historical note.
Correct the status to current.
Remove the Trello link.
Exclude PL 16-117 from permanent Code.

laws.title: Public Law 16-118 | The Uniformed Capitol Police Act of 2023
laws.final_statutory_text: Repeals H.R. 8013 Section III and replaces that section with a new Capitol Police Board structure and powers.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; the source is intact and operative, and nothing repeals PL 16-118 itself.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Direct repeal and substitution of H.R. 8013 Section III; the change must be applied to the prior section text.
laws.status_basis: Source is intact and operative. It repeals H.R. 8013 Section III and replaces that section with a new Capitol Police Board structure and powers. Nothing repeals PL 16-118.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note sits in title 18, but the operative target is Capitol Police governance under title 2. The repeal and replacement of H.R. 8013 Section III are not implemented.

recommended_actions: Apply the repeal/substitution to the Capitol Police Board text at the title 2 anchor.
Re-anchor from title 18 to title 2.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 17-124 | Law Enforcement Accountability Act (LEAA)
laws.final_statutory_text: Creates use-of-force, canine, and de-escalation rules for law enforcement as a generally applicable regulatory statute.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; the full source is present, and no self-repeal or sunset was identified in the reviewed materials.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer issue; the act is a substantive regulatory framework and amends no numbered USAR Code section by express strike/insert.
laws.status_basis: Full source present. The act is a free-standing, generally applicable regulatory statute for law enforcement, with no self-repeal or sunset and no express strike/insert amendment.

current_implementation.detail: The draft added only a boilerplate note at title 18 and declared the act current. Because this is substantive permanent general law, it should be enacted as real Code section(s), not merely a note.

recommended_actions: Codify as a new permanent chapter or sections in the appropriate law-enforcement placement rather than a bare note.
Remove the Trello link from the codification entry.
Correct duplicated SEC5 numbering and the missing SEC6 in editorial notes.
